dimensions.note: Credential resolution stays delegated to the AWS SDK (fromIni/ambient) via the aws_credentials twin; no secrets vaulted or logged. The inline preflightSso wiring is replaced with the shared preflightSsoGate — same injectable token resolver, only the remediation message (no token bytes) surfaces. The known credentialModeField 'looks like a secret' WARN is the documented false positive (an enum of 'profile'/'env', not a secret).

The two pre-flight warn messages moved verbatim into the shared gate (asserted byte-for-byte by the canonical capturing-logger tests in _lib/aws_credentials_test.ts). The caller keeps its own 'iam-role-audit complete' info summary with role/present/error counts on the abort path. Structured placeholders throughout.

Policy unchanged, now owned by the gate: expired aborts with one actionable scan_error, network warns and proceeds, empty ssoSession skips inside the gate. This audit's role-scoped row shape is preserved by design — the abort row spreads the canonical fragment over {profile:'', accountId:'', roleName:''} (no region field), exactly what the decision-returning gate interface was chosen for. Existing pre-flight unit tests pass unchanged.

The runScan pre-flight paths (expired short-circuit, network proceed, no-session no-op) remain pinned by the existing unit/smoke suites, which pass unchanged against the gate. This package never had a resolveBootstrapRegion copy (global IAM service takes a region argument), so no test relocation applies; the gate policy itself is covered once in the canonical _lib suite.
Audit remains read-only and re-runnable with deterministic keys. The gate is resolve-only; empty ssoSession preserves prior behavior exactly.

No AWS request/response mapping changed; SHARED_RETRY still spread into the IAM/CFN/STS clients; SDK pins unchanged at 3.1073.0. This package intentionally gains no resolveBootstrapRegion (global service, explicit region argument) — left alone per the migration scope.
No new clients, handles, or files. The gate opens no SDK client of its own; existing cleanup paths untouched.
Manifest and model.version aligned at 2026.07.03.0. README unchanged (behavior identical; the preflight wiring is internal). Test fixtures keep placeholder identifiers.

MECHANICAL Schema-Write Conformance re-verified at the changed call site: the abort-path writeError spreads the typed PreflightSsoErrorFragment over {profile:'', accountId:'', roleName:''} and appends scannedAt — all 8 fields of this package's role-scoped ScanError present (profile, accountId, roleName, service, phase, kind, message, scannedAt), none extra; the 'auth_expired' literal narrows into ScanErrorKind. Schemas remain strict z.object, no passthrough.
role/scan_error keep lifetime 'infinite' and garbageCollection 10 — unchanged.
Read-only fan-out audit, not a CRUD lifecycle model. Unchanged by this slice.
SSO pre-flight still opt-in and runs once before the per-profile loop; the deps.resolveSsoToken seam is preserved through the gate. requiredProfileSuffix refusal still fires before any AWS call.

MECHANICAL: key construction unchanged — this package keys scan_error by scanErrorKey(profile, roleName, phase); the preflight abort row keys identically to before (empty prefix + preflight_sso phase). Role row keys unchanged.
writeResource-only producer; no readResource paths changed. Downstream consumers see byte-identical row shapes.

model.version bumped 2026.06.26.1 -> 2026.07.03.0 with a matching no-op upgrade entry appended (identity upgradeAttributes; description notes the centralized pre-flight, no globalArguments schema changes). Chain tail equals model.version, verified by the colocated manifest-sync test.
